jokes.text: Why are we watching the Business Channel? Because the other day I saw there was a stock with my initials, MEG, on it.
jokes.setup: Question about why they're watching business news
jokes.punchline: Monica watches for a stock that happens to match her initials
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdity showing Monica's need for control/order extending to random stock watching

jokes.text: Sometimes it takes two or three hours before it comes up again. But when it does, it's exciting.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her stock watching habit
jokes.punchline: She waits hours for a random stock to appear just because it has her initials
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity while perfectly capturing Monica's obsessive personality

jokes.text: Honey, you really need a job.
jokes.setup: Monica's bizarre stock-watching behavior
jokes.punchline: Rachel's matter-of-fact assessment that Monica needs employment
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response with economy and timing

jokes.text: On behalf of everyone, I'd just like to say, 'Huh?'
jokes.setup: Phoebe talking about signs from her father
jokes.punchline: Chandler formally representing group confusion
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - formal language for informal situation, speaks for audience confusion

jokes.text: Which is my father's last name... And they were serving franks... which is his first name, minus the 's' at the end.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining signs from the universe
jokes.punchline: Incredibly tenuous connection between 'franks' and 'Frank'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - elaborate reasoning for ridiculous connections

jokes.text: His Indian name?
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions spinning chicken as a sign
jokes.punchline: Chandler assumes it's about a Native American name rather than fear
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection - audience and Chandler both misinterpret 'chicken'

jokes.text: Hamburger? McDonald's? Old McDonald had a farm. My dad is a pharmacist.
jokes.setup: Someone offering the last hamburger
jokes.punchline: Increasingly tenuous chain of associations ending with pharmacist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Phoebe's sign-seeing, each connection more ridiculous than the last

jokes.text: Ah.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's completely insane chain of reasoning
jokes.punchline: Group's collective defeated acceptance of Phoebe logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect beat - the group's resignation to Phoebe's worldview

jokes.text: Okay. There is one more way to say it. Who knows it?
jokes.setup: Bullies demanding their seats back aggressively
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating confrontation like a game show
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflection - treating serious situation with game show host energy

jokes.text: Is that supposed to be funny? Actually, I was going for colorful.
jokes.setup: Bully challenging Chandler's humor
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pretentious defense of his joke
jokes.explanation: Chandler's intellectual pretension in the face of physical threat

jokes.text: I just took your hat. See, I can be funny too. My joke is that I took your hat.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to be funny with bullies
jokes.punchline: Bully's complete misunderstanding of how humor works
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-humor - bully explaining why theft equals comedy

jokes.text: You know what? I think you're very funny. Kudos on that hat joke.
jokes.setup: Bully stealing hat and calling it comedy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's overly polite sarcastic praise
jokes.explanation: Chandler's passive-aggressive response fits character but isn't exceptional

jokes.text: Because it's a special hat. He bought it because he was feeling down one day. So he got the hat to cheer himself up, you know?
jokes.setup: Trying to get Chandler's hat back from bullies
jokes.punchline: Ross's oversharing backstory that makes situation worse
jokes.explanation: Ross's well-intentioned but counterproductive oversharing makes things worse

jokes.text: Stop talking. Stop talking now.
jokes.setup: Ross making the hat situation worse with TMI
jokes.punchline: Chandler's desperate attempt to stop the damage
jokes.explanation: Good payoff beat to Ross's oversharing, economical and relatable

jokes.text: Let me get this straight. You're stealing my hat? You got a problem? I just want to make sure we're on the same page.
jokes.setup: Bully threatening Chandler over hat theft clarification
jokes.punchline: Chandler's business-like approach to being mugged
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - treating mugging like a business transaction

jokes.text: How can I take a job where I have to make Laverne-and-Curly Fries?
jokes.setup: Monica describing degrading theme restaurant job
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous menu item combining TV show with food
jokes.explanation: Solid pun showing Monica's professional pride vs. financial desperation

jokes.text: Monica, relax. Go get a beer. I don't want a beer. Who said it was for you?
jokes.setup: Joey telling stressed Monica to get a beer
jokes.punchline: Revealing the beer suggestion was for Joey himself
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with perfect Joey selfishness, economical and surprising

jokes.text: Oh, hi. Oh... Oh, no, wait a minute. I have no one.
jokes.setup: Monica about to greet someone romantically
jokes.punchline: Realizing mid-greeting she's actually alone
jokes.explanation: Monica's loneliness played for laughs with good timing

jokes.text: Forget it. It's probably stripped and sold for parts by now.
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting they get Chandler's hat back
jokes.punchline: Treating a hat like a stolen car
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation treating hat theft like grand theft auto

jokes.text: Do you realize that if I'd invested my $127 in myself yesterday... that I'd, like, have a lot more than that today.
jokes.setup: Monica's stock going up
jokes.punchline: Vague understanding of how investment gains work
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but Monica's financial naivety isn't as sharp as other character moments

jokes.text: What's to know? Buy, sell. High, low. Bears, bulls. Yes, Manhattan. Telephone number to the stock... selling store.
jokes.setup: Monica claiming to understand the stock market
jokes.punchline: Escalating ignorance ending with 'stock selling store'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with 'stock selling store' as perfect capstone to Monica's ignorance

jokes.text: No mayo. No, because mayo, that would make it gross.
jokes.setup: Joey describing his disgusting sandwich ingredients
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically noting mayo would be the problem
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic reaction to Joey's already disgusting sandwich combo

jokes.text: Get the hell off my leg, you yippity piece of crap!
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to love all animals
jokes.punchline: Her patience snapping with creative profanity at small dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect character break - peaceful Phoebe losing it with 'yippity piece of crap'

jokes.text: The dog will lick himself but he will not touch your sandwich. What does that tell you?
jokes.setup: Dog refusing to eat Joey's sandwich
jokes.punchline: Pointing out dog's hygiene standards vs. food standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about animal behavior vs. Joey's gross food

jokes.text: Do you have to be a Century 21 real-estate agent to get to wear those cool jackets?
jokes.setup: Random question after intense dog situation
jokes.punchline: Joey's complete non-sequitur about real estate jackets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey randomness - completely off-topic materialistic observation

jokes.text: Do you say this stuff to girls?
jokes.setup: Joey's random Century 21 comment
jokes.punchline: Rachel's incredulous reaction to Joey's thought process
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response but not particularly original

jokes.text: I'll tell you what, you call the couch, and then we'll call the couch, and we'll see who it comes to.
jokes.setup: Bullies claiming the couch belongs to them
jokes.punchline: Treating couch ownership like calling a pet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic, perfectly delivered Chandler line with great structure and quotability

jokes.text: We didn't want to have to go and do that.
jokes.setup: Gunther easily resolving the bully situation
jokes.punchline: Ross acting tough after someone else solved the problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's false bravado after the fact

jokes.text: Let's take this outside.
jokes.setup: Escalating confrontation with bullies
jokes.punchline: Bully using classic tough guy cliche
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next joke but itself relies on overused tough guy trope

jokes.text: Who talks like that?
jokes.setup: Bully using 'let's take this outside' line
jokes.punchline: Ross questioning the cliche as unrealistic
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on tough guy movie cliches, very economical

jokes.text: The guy who's about to kick your ass talks like that. You had to ask.
jokes.setup: Ross questioning the tough guy language
jokes.punchline: Bully's matter-of-fact response and Chandler's regret
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Ross's question with Chandler's typical regret

jokes.text: I think you played the Gunther card too soon.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler now banned from coffeehouse after Gunther helped
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating Gunther like a strategic resource
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler analysis of their strategic mistake, treating social interaction like a game

jokes.text: I just think that this was a really bad sign. Like the beast at the threshold.
jokes.setup: Dog attacking her at father's house
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's mystical interpretation of dog attack
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe mysticism but 'beast at the threshold' feels slightly forced

jokes.text: And I have a sister who I've barely spoken to since we shared a womb.
jokes.setup: Phoebe lamenting her lack of family
jokes.punchline: Unique way of describing twin sister estrangement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, unique phrasing for sibling estrangement - 'shared a womb' is both funny and touching

jokes.text: Uh, I'm guessing the threshold's clear now.
jokes.setup: Dog yelping and running away after being hit by car
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dark callback to Phoebe's 'beast at threshold'
jokes.explanation: Good dark callback, economical and well-timed

jokes.text: Time is money, my friend! You missed 'Takes money to make money' and 'Don't make me come down there and kick your Wall Street butt.'
jokes.setup: Monica acting like a stock trader
jokes.punchline: Chandler listing other cliches she missed
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of Monica's stock trader persona with Chandler's list

jokes.text: I had breakfast here, so technically I saved $3.50.
jokes.setup: Monica bragging about making $17
jokes.punchline: Chandler's alternative accounting of coffee shop breakfast
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler logic deflating Monica's excitement with alternative perspective

jokes.text: Well, CHP because I used to have a crush on Erik Estrada. And ZXY because I think it sounds 'zexy.'
jokes.setup: Asked why she picked certain stocks
jokes.punchline: Completely non-financial reasoning based on TV crushes and wordplay
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic for stock picking, 'zexy' pronunciation is memorable

jokes.text: MEG was good for me, but I dumped her. My motto is, 'Get out before they go down.' That is so not my motto.
jokes.setup: Monica talking about dumping her MEG stock
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo followed by Rachel's mortified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre setup with Rachel's perfect reaction, though innuendo is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: And he said that only once in a blue moon does a dog's ear grow back. So still hoping.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining dog will be okay after injury
jokes.punchline: Casuallydelivering horrible news about permanent ear damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe dark humor delivery - treating horrible news casually with false hope

jokes.text: What will I say, 'Hi, I'm the daughter you abandoned. Oh, by the way, I broke your dog.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe not wanting to call her father about his injured dog
jokes.punchline: Imagining worst possible way to introduce herself
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor combining abandonment issues with immediate practical problem

jokes.text: I know where your dog is. I want you to know he'll be returned to you almost as good as new within 24 hours. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Ross calling Phoebe's father about the dog
jokes.punchline: Making it sound like a ransom call
jokes.explanation: Ross accidentally making innocent dog return sound like kidnapping threat

jokes.text: Why the voice? Hard to say.
jokes.setup: Monica asking why Ross used a weird voice on the phone
jokes.punchline: Ross's non-explanation for his bizarre choice
jokes.explanation: Decent beat showing Ross's inability to explain his awkward choices

jokes.text: The package says to keep it moving. Stir and drink, never let it settle.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why instant cappuccino isn't mixing
jokes.punchline: Ross's frantic stirring instructions revealing inferior product
jokes.explanation: Shows the pathetic coffee substitute situation but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: And I don't want to drink cappuccino with a 'k.'
jokes.setup: Discussing their coffee house exile
jokes.punchline: Chandler's snobbery about instant cappuccino spelling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler pretension - caring about spelling of coffee substitutes

jokes.text: Ross, have you ever been beaten up before? Yeah, sure. By someone besides Monica? No.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about Ross's fighting experience
jokes.punchline: Revealing Monica as Ross's only opponent
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing sibling dynamic, perfect structure and character insight

jokes.text: Couldn't we just lose our virginities again? Because I think actually mine's growing back.
jokes.setup: Ross comparing fighting to a rite of passage
jokes.punchline: Suggesting sexual inexperience is returning from lack of use
jokes.explanation: Great self-deprecating humor about Ross's lack of sexual activity

jokes.text: I don't want to have to wear flame-retardant boobs.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why she needs money to avoid diner job
jokes.punchline: Specific ridiculous detail about restaurant costume safety
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific absurd detail - 'flame-retardant boobs' is both ridiculous and quotable

jokes.text: Nobody does, honey.
jokes.setup: Monica not wanting to wear flame-retardant boobs
jokes.punchline: Rachel's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Good supportive beat but doesn't add much beyond agreement

jokes.text: Yeah, eight of them. That's 56 to him.
jokes.setup: Woman asking about dog's stitches
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dog years calculation for stitches
jokes.explanation: Clever application of dog years concept to injury severity - uniquely Phoebe logic

jokes.text: If it's raining, you can't let him look up too long because that cone will fill up fast.
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving care instructions for injured dog
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about cone collar filling with rainwater
jokes.explanation: Absurdly practical concern showing Phoebe's unique way of thinking through problems

jokes.text: He left four years ago. We're expecting him back any minute.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if Frank Senior will be back from groceries soon
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been gone for years but still optimistic
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about abandonment delivered matter-of-factly, great misdirection

jokes.text: One time, I was upstairs stealing cigarettes out of my mom's purse, and all of a sudden I look over, and there's Dad's head, bobbing past the window.
jokes.setup: Frank Jr. explaining father's love of stilts
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of father on stilts appearing at second-story window
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy - the casual mention of stealing cigarettes adds to the absurdity

jokes.text: But you know what's cool? If you had a friend named Pete, I could say: 'I know Pete. He's friends with my brother.'
jokes.setup: Frank Jr. excited about having a sister
jokes.punchline: Very specific hypothetical social scenario he's excited about
jokes.explanation: Sweet and absurd - Frank Jr.'s excitement about mundane social connections

jokes.text: If you want, I can show you where he hit his head on the rain gutter.
jokes.setup: Phoebe and Frank Jr. bonding over their father
jokes.punchline: Offering to show evidence of father's stilt accident
jokes.explanation: Good dark callback to stilt story, treating injury site as tourist attraction

jokes.text: Bullies! Big bullies!
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler confronting the coffee house bullies
jokes.punchline: Ross's childish name-calling as intimidation tactic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's lack of fighting skills, but not particularly original

jokes.text: A nice watch. I don't want to break it on your ribs.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if bully has a weapon
jokes.punchline: Bully's considerate concern for his watch during violence
jokes.explanation: Decent irony about caring for possessions while threatening violence

jokes.text: Question: If I don't care about my watch, can I use it as a weapon? It's sharp, it's metal. I think I can do some serious damage with it.
jokes.setup: Pre-fight weapons discussion
jokes.punchline: Chandler's analytical approach to improvised weapons
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler moment - overthinking fighting with corporate mindset

jokes.text: Of course. Why wouldn't you hit faces? I have to work on Monday. I have a big presentation.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about hitting faces
jokes.punchline: Chandler's work concerns about visible bruising
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler prioritizing work presentation over physical integrity

jokes.text: Actually, I gotta show this apartment tomorrow, and this no-faces thing might not be a bad idea. Dana's ovulating. Oh, really? You guys trying again?
jokes.setup: Agreeing to no face-hitting rule
jokes.punchline: Intimate ovulation details shared with strangers mid-fight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from violence to TMI ovulation discussion

jokes.text: Let me get this straight. We're talking about the middle?
jokes.setup: Agreeing no hits above neck or below waist
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifying they're only fighting torsos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler logic - reducing fight to its most ridiculous restrictions

jokes.text: Those guys are taking our stuff! Hey, hey!
jokes.setup: Big fight buildup with complicated rules
jokes.punchline: Fight immediately derailed by petty theft
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax but the setup was stronger than the payoff

jokes.text: Any one of us could have tripped over that little girl's jump rope.
jokes.setup: Ross embarrassed about missing the fight
jokes.punchline: Joey's condescending comfort about tripping over child's toy
jokes.explanation: Good Joey attempt at comfort that actually makes Ross feel worse

jokes.text: So can I have my hat back? No. Huh.
jokes.setup: After seemingly resolving conflict with bullies
jokes.punchline: Hat still not returned despite friendly resolution
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the pointless nature of the whole conflict - perfect anticlimax

jokes.text: Hi, Monica! How's it going? Hey, nice boobs.
jokes.setup: Monica working at the themed diner
jokes.punchline: Customer complimenting her costume boobs
jokes.explanation: Decent visual payoff to earlier setup but fairly predictable

jokes.text: It's fun to stay at the YMCA
jokes.setup: Monica forced to perform at themed restaurant
jokes.punchline: Monica having to sing and dance to Village People
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's professional fall but relies on inherent comedy of YMCA rather than original writing
